mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly suppressing valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack accelerates and signals imminent economic deceleration, triggering negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment remains below 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive quarterly equity returns through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in spending and housing fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During active rate hiking periods, positive industrial production growth confirms resilient supply-side capacity that offsets financial headwinds, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when rates are stable.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion, generating positive forward equity returns over a three-month horizon as earnings adjust to macroeconomic output.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP revisions fail to predict positive 60-day equity returns with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 310.0, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of periods where CPI exceeds 310.0, or the threshold effect disappears when inflation expectations are anchored below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand absorption, driving positive monthly equity returns through wage-driven consumption channels.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55 percent of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when payroll growth falls below 100 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: In stagflationary environments, rising consumer prices alongside stagnant real spending erode real household purchasing power, triggering immediate negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of stagflationary periods, or the negative interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.
